mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL > 280) signals central bank intervention through rate hikes, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic expansion, driving corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in co-expansionary periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) create a 'stagflation' risk environment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in tightening/inflationary regimes exceed 10% annualized, or Rank IC > 0.15 in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high-payroll growth periods exceed 2% in > 40% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE < 3.5%) signals labor market overheating, prompting monetary policy tightening and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods exceed 5% annualized in > 30% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand-side momentum, driving corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in co-demand expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 100) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) indicates supply-side shocks, creating stagflationary pressures and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in supply-demand mismatch periods exceed 3% in > 25% of cases, or Rank IC > 0.10 in such periods.
